Through real spex issues + real git (throwaway clone): open mints id 'store-lock-must-serialize-rmw' + --no-verify store commit on trunk; 'spex issues' lists it store-tagged local, by sess-A, re: local-issues; a 2nd session sign/reply/resolve each report success; final --json shows store=local, by=sess-A, status=accepted, evidence hash, signer sess-B, reply(by/at/body). Every write round-trips through the unified read. (resolve requires --as <accepted|rejected|landed>.)

(a) 8 concurrent 'issues open' + 8 concurrent 'issues reply' at one thread → all 8 opens committed (8 new store files), all 8 replies landed (none lost to RMW race), 16 --no-verify commits, git tree clean (nothing left uncommitted): withStoreLock serializes the whole read-mutate-write-commit. (b) a body line that IS a reply sentinel is neutralized on write (safeBody inserts a ZWSP → '<!--\u200breply:'): 0 phantom replies, after-sentinel text survives (no truncation), a genuine reply still parses (count→1). spex lint = 0 error throughout.

With 48 threads under .spec/.issues/ (all plain <id>.md, 0 spec.md): spex lint = 0 error (80 pre-existing warnings), no lint line references any store thread. spex board = 147 nodes, 0 store ids leak as nodes (forgery-probe/burst-target/store-lock all absent). The walk never nodes a non-spec.md path and isSpecMd ignores it — the store is structurally invisible to lint/drift/deriveStatus with NO special-case exemption.

On the trunk with the pre-commit hook active: (1) 'spex issues open' lands a store commit because the writer commits --no-verify — bypasses the hook, needs no guard exception. (2) a plain non-store commit is BLOCKED ('direct commits on main are blocked'). (3) a plain store-only commit (no --no-verify) is blocked TOO — main-guard carries no .spec/.issues special-case, so it stays the single clean question 'am I committing directly onto the trunk?'. (4) SPEXCODE_ALLOW_MAIN=1 lets it through, proving the guard is the blocker.

Default (no config) = ON. 'spex issues off' writes issues.enabled:false to spexcode.json → status OFF, the next --no-ff node merge prints NO post-merge nudge and 'spex issues nudge <node>' is empty. 'spex issues on' restores the nudge on the following merge (names the node). spexcode.json is the single source of truth and a pre-rename proposals.enabled:false still reads as OFF. The git post-merge hook honors the switch through the CLI.
